title: Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Rajasthan

overview.short: Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in southeastern Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in southeastern Rajasthan, India. It was notified on 9 October 1975 and covers an area of approximately 194.6 km2 (75.1 sq mi), spanning the districts of Kota, Bundi, and Chittorgarh along the Chambal River. The sanctuary forms part of the buffer zone of the Mukundara Hills Tiger Reserve, which was notified in 2013 under Project Tiger.

topPlaces.name: Jawahar Sagar Dam
topPlaces.description: The Jawahar Sagar Dam is the third dam in the series of Chambal Valley Projects on the Chambal River, located 29 km upstream of Kota city and 26 km downstream of Rana Pratap Sagar dam. It is a concrete gravity dam, 45 meters high and 393 meters long, generating 60 MW of power with an installed capacity of 3 units of 33 MW. Its construction was completed in 1972.

topPlaces.name: Baroli Temples
topPlaces.description: The Baroli Temples Complex, also known as the Badoli temples, is located in Baroli village in Rawatbhata City in Chittorgarh district of Rajasthan, India. The complex of eight temples is situated within a walled enclosure; an additional temple is about 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) away.

topPlaces.name: Ghateshwar Temple
topPlaces.description: Ghateshwar Temple is a Hindu temple located in Badoli (also spelled Baroli), one of the nine temples near the river Chambal in Rajasthan, India. The Ghateshwar temple is dedicated to the god Shiva and is constructed in the Pratihara style of circa.

items.note: The Jawahar Sagar Dam is the third dam in the series of Chambal Valley Projects on the Chambal River, located 29 km upstream of Kota city and 26 km downstream of Rana Pratap Sagar dam. It is a concrete gravity dam, 45 meters high and 393 meters long, generating 60 MW of power with an installed capacity of 3 units of 33 MW. Its construction was completed in 1972.

The Baroli Temples Complex, also known as the Badoli temples, is located in Baroli village in Rawatbhata City in Chittorgarh district of Rajasthan, India. The complex of eight temples is situated within a walled enclosure; an additional temple is about 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) away.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kota Airport (27 km) and the nearest railway station is Kota Junction (29 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jawahar Sagar Dam, Baroli Temples, Ghateshwar Temple.
